goal: Implement authenticated GitHub REST API requests in a Node backend
acceptanceCriteria: Uses a server-side environment variable for the token and never exposes it in frontend code.
Includes required REST headers (Authorization Bearer token, Accept, and X-GitHub-Api-Version).
Provides robust non-2xx handling with actionable error messages for 401 and 403/404 permission cases.
Returns a reusable request helper for future endpoints instead of one-off snippets.
securityRequirements: Never expose PATs in browser code, logs, or committed files.
Prefer least-privilege tokens and mention permission-scope mismatch as a failure mode.

goal: Create an issue in a repository from a Node backend service
acceptanceCriteria: Builds a POST request to /repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues with at least title and optional body.
Sends JSON correctly with Content-Type and Authorization headers.
Validates critical input (owner/repo/title) before request execution.
Handles repository visibility and permission failures (403/404) in a developer-friendly way.
securityRequirements: Keep token and repo secrets server-side only.
Do not suggest storing tokens in localStorage or frontend bundles.

goal: Validate GitHub webhook signatures in a Node server before processing payloads
acceptanceCriteria: Uses the webhook secret to compute HMAC SHA-256 over the raw request body.
Compares expected and received signatures using timing-safe comparison.
Rejects invalid signatures with a clear error response before business logic runs.
Highlights framework caveat that parsed JSON can break signature checks if raw body is not preserved.
securityRequirements: Never process webhook payloads before signature verification.
Never log secrets or full sensitive payloads in production logs.
